name: Web Graph
description: Standard for the graph / node-link visualization domain — a sibling of Web Charts in the viz family. A graph is declared by its semantic intent (the nodes and edges and what they mean) kept strictly separate from its presentation (how it is laid out and drawn, consuming Web Theme tokens). What sets a graph apart from a chart is a positioning degree of freedom: a chart derives a mark's position from its datum via scales, but a graph invents position via a layout algorithm — so the renderer seam splits in two: a CustomGraphLayout Protocol (spec → positioned coordinates) ⊥ a CustomGraphRenderer Protocol (positioned + theme → output), with a deterministic layered-DAG layout and a native-first SVG renderer as the defaults so adapters (D3-force, ELK/dagre, Cytoscape) plug in behind the contracts. Conformance scores three axes — semantic fidelity, theme application, and accessibility (a first-class axis, with an adjacency description-table floor). Shape ratified in #1352 and scaffolded under epic #1351.
summary: Declarative node-link graphs that separate layout from rendering, with swappable engines.
tierEvidence: Frontier UI ships the graph renderer at graphs/SvgGraphRenderer.ts + LayeredDagLayout.ts — a shipped constellation consumer of the node-link viz protocol.
